core_innovation.name: GhostGate
core_innovation.description: GhostGate replaces SiLU activations in transformer MLP blocks with a thresholded variant that hard-zeros activations below a configurable threshold. This creates structured activation sparsity without retraining.

cold_relu_surgery.finding: Hard ReLU replacement causes representation collapse

key_findings.evidence: Sparsity scales predictably from 34% to 70% with threshold
key_findings.implication: We have a dial to control the sparsity-quality tradeoff

key_findings.finding: Sparsity robustness scales with model size
key_findings.evidence: 1B collapses at t=0.20, 7B holds 10/10 coherence at t=0.25
key_findings.implication: Larger models are better candidates for GhostWeight streaming

key_findings.evidence: t=0.20 degrades to 3/5 but t=0.25 recovers to 5/5
key_findings.implication: Critical neurons exist in specific activation ranges. Above that range they are suppressed naturally anyway.

key_findings.finding: 70.28% of 7B model neurons are expendable per forward pass
key_findings.evidence: Qwen2.5-7B at t=0.25 produces 10/10 coherent outputs
key_findings.implication: Only 29.72% of weights need to be active in VRAM at any moment. Phase 2 will stream only these active weights.

phase2_plan.goal: Stream only active 29.72% of weights per layer
phase2_plan.method: Blackwell async copy engine + sparse weight index
phase2_plan.target: Run a model 3-10x larger than VRAM capacity
